PETITION For 1. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties By Former Attorney In Fact; 2. Financial Elder Abuse; 3.
Off Calendar Apr-15-2025 Continued To Aug-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Vincent Yu appeared via zoom for Gene Brown (petitioner).
Martin Reed appeared via zoom for Perry Brown (respondent).

Discussion was held regarding Mr. Brown allegedly move funds in the amount of $173,000.00, that there are outstanding depositions that need to take place, that they need more time for discovery, and that they are requesting a 90 to 120 days continuance of the hearing. Petition for breach of fiduciary duties by former attorney in fact is continued to August 19, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of mediation is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

PETITION Petition For 1. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties By Former Attorney In Fact; 2. Financial Elder Abuse; 3. Compel Accoutning By Former Attorney In Fact; And 4. Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Continued To Apr-15-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Vincent Yu for Gene Brown, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Martin Reed for Perry Brown, Respondent (via Zoom).

Counsel reported that mediation occurred but was unsuccessful due to outstanding discovery issues. Mr. Yu stated that financial records from Wells Fargo Bank and Morgan Stanley are critical for resolution. Mr. Reed indicated that his client is not obligated to provide the records as they pertain to the deceased's estate, not the respondent directly. The court expressed frustration at the inability to resolve the discovery issues and noted the case does not appear overly complicated. Counsel were encouraged to cooperate with each other to obtain necessary records and proceed to mediation.

The court continued the matter to April 15, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 for further updates.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Continued To Apr-15-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For 1. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties By Former Attorney In Fact; 2. Financial Elder Abuse; 3. Compel Accoutning By Former Attorney In Fact; And 4. Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-02-2024 Continued To Jan-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vincent Yu appeared by zoom for and with Gene Brown by zoom.
Martin Reed appeared by zoom for Perry Brown (respondent).

The court asked if the parties have met and conferred and whether they have a road map for resolution. Mr. Reed stated that the only thing he has received is discovery and other than that, he has heard nothing from the petitioner. Mr. Yu stated that many of their denials are problematic, and that it is appropriate to allow time for discovery and to set the matter for an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Yu also stated that his client has received $50,000.00 from the trust, that during the time that Carmen was alive, they believe there may have been some pay on death accounts, and they believe Perry may have taken the money. Gene stated that he was told by his mother that it was $80,000.00. At the court's suggestion, the Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than December 31, 2024. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 7, 2025. The Petition for Breach of Fiduciary Duties by Former Attorney-in-Fact, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on January 14, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties are to obtain a copy of the mediation order from the court's website.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Wong. Not reported.

PETITION Petition For 1. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties By Former Attorney In Fact; 2. Financial Elder Abuse; 3. Compel Accoutning By Former Attorney In Fact; And
Off Calendar Aug-21-2024 Continued To Oct-02-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello appeared by zoom for Gene Brown.
Martin Reed appeared by zoom for Harry Brown (respondent).

Mr. Reed stated that a responsive pleading was just filed in the last few minutes. The court asked if the parties have met and conferred, and whether they have a road map for resolution. Mr. Reed and Mrs. Robello both stated that they need more time. The Petition for Breach of Fiduciary Duties by Former Attorney-in-Fact, etc. is continued to October 2, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mrs. Robello is to file a response by September 18, 2024.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Magdaleno. Not reported.
